title: Repo inspiration / attribution manifest — Computer-Use Repo-Backed Adapter
policy: Design patterns are adapted from computer-use repos statically inspected in N+6.12A. No third-party code is vendored or committed. Repos were shallow-cloned in N+6.12A into 21_repos/third_party_static/ (gitignored). For N+6.29A no new clones are performed; the N+6.12A static inspection record is used as the authoritative source.

repos.name: TryCUA / CUA Driver
patterns_adapted: structured action-intent payload: each proposed action carries a declared type, target, and value
sandbox isolation boundary: actions validated against an allowed-target list before execution
capability declaration: plan must declare required capabilities; blocked capabilities cause rejection
approval gate: human approval token required before any real execution
dry-run status payload: adapter emits a structured result with all real_*_performed fields false
risk_notes: live desktop control via Docker/QEMU/KASM — not used
189 shell scripts — none inspected for execution
real OS input via OS-level APIs — hard-blocked in Ghoti adapter
repos.status: patterns only; not installed, not executed; no code copied

repos.name: UI-TARS
repos.inspected_in: N+6.12A (source-needed — no confirmed URL recorded; patterns from prior N+5.0A context)
patterns_adapted: observation-only mode: adapter can inspect/read state without performing real OS actions
structured action contract: each action is a typed record (click, type, read) with explicit field separation
blocked-action manifest: the adapter maintains a canonical list of refused action types
arena status block: result includes a simulation: true / live_execution: false block for visualization
risk_notes: real GUI automation via OS-level APIs — not used
model inference — not used; adapter is stdlib-only
repos.status: patterns from N+5.0A context and N+6.12A inspection record; no code copied; no execution

repos.name: Browser Harness
patterns_adapted: local fixture pattern: use a static JSON fixture as the action target instead of a live page
CDP isolation: browser actions confined to a local loopback channel (127.0.0.1)
blocked-URL guard: adapter checks target_url against allowed-prefix list before accepting plan
risk_notes: self-writing-code risk — not used; adapter writes no code
CDP to real web pages — blocked; adapter only allows file:// and localhost URLs
repos.status: patterns only; not installed, not executed; no code copied

repos.name: Vercel agent-browser
patterns_adapted: capability declaration contract: the plan declares capabilities_required; the adapter validates each against a blocked set
approval gate shape: approval_token field in result; null until human approves
refused_live_actions list: canonical list of refused actions surfaced in every result payload
repos.license_note: Apache-2.0 requires NOTICE file only if code is copied. No code is copied in this milestone.
repos.status: patterns only; not installed, not executed; no code copied; no NOTICE needed

repos.name: Ruflo
patterns_adapted: coordinator/worker + shared-local-memory hand-off pattern: adapter acts as coordinator; Rust policy checker (N+6.28A) acts as downstream worker
declared-skill pattern: adapter explicitly declares which action types it allows and which it refuses
dry-run-first invariant: no real execution without an explicit flag AND human approval
risk_notes: supply-chain history: obfuscated malicious npm pre-install (v3.1.0-alpha.55..v3.5.2); MCP prompt-injection #1375; SQL-injection — not used; no Ruflo code vendored
install scripts: install.sh, postinstall hooks — not run
repos.status: patterns only (re-expressed from scratch); not installed, not executed; no code copied

prior_milestones_used_as_reference.role: Primary static inspection source for all five repos above
Confined browser sandbox + CDP isolation pattern reference
Repo-backed manifest + repo inspiration pattern (unmerged; no files from this branch modified)
Rust policy checker target for future bridge (unmerged; no files from this branch modified)

rust_policy_bridge.bridge_note: N+6.28A Rust policy checker is not yet merged. The adapter result includes rust_policy_bridge_ready: false. Post-merge, the adapter should pipe the validated plan to `cargo run --manifest-path rust/ghoti_policy_checker/Cargo.toml` for a second-pass policy check.

attribution_note: The Ghoti computer-use adapter is original code (stdlib-only Python) that re-expresses the public design patterns listed above. For MIT/Apache-2.0 repos, any future small permitted snippet reuse would carry the copyright notice; no snippet is reused in this milestone.
